likely_root_causes: The target load likely filtered out valid source members or the curated insert step missed a subset of records.
The parent-child remapping logic is likely outdated relative to the Smart View source hierarchy.
Hierarchy depth appears to have been recalculated incorrectly during normalization or flattening.
The normalized source and target hierarchy rows do not align, which usually points to sheet-selection, normalization, or load logic drift.
Numeric measure data differs between source and target, which usually points to an aggregation, currency, or row-level load mismatch.
recommended_actions: Re-run the target load for the missing members and review any filters applied during normalization.
Compare the mapping logic to the current source hierarchy and correct the parent remap rules.
Recalculate hierarchy levels from the resolved parent-child structure during normalization.
Compare normalized source and target rows side by side and reconcile any missing, extra, or structurally different hierarchy rows.
Compare source and target measure rows on the shared business keys, then reconcile any mismatched numeric values or missing measure rows.
agent_explanation:
### What Was Tested
- Entity - Missing members in target, Entity - Parent existence, Entity - Source parent vs target parent mismatch, Entity - Duplicate members, Entity - Leaf/non-leaf consistency, Entity - Level consistency, Entity - Row-level hierarchy match, Entity - Numeric value match

### What Failed
- Entity - Missing members in target (15 failed records), Entity - Source parent vs target parent mismatch (1 failed records), Entity - Level consistency (1 failed records), Entity - Row-level hierarchy match (63 failed records)
- 5 failed checks were recorded and 85 records were flagged.

### Likely Cause
- The target load likely filtered out valid source members or the curated insert step missed a subset of records., The parent-child remapping logic is likely outdated relative to the Smart View source hierarchy., Hierarchy depth appears to have been recalculated incorrectly during normalization or flattening.

### Recommended Action
- Re-run the target load for the missing members and review any filters applied during normalization., Compare the mapping logic to the current source hierarchy and correct the parent remap rules., Recalculate hierarchy levels from the resolved parent-child structure during normalization.
